reasons: Atlas can group enemies, giving Pharsa a clear long-range damage window.
Pharsa can follow Atlas from safer distance instead of standing inside the engage.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped crowd-control setup, while Pharsa punishes the trapped enemies from long range.
worksBestWhen: Atlas waits until Pharsa can follow.
Pharsa saves damage for Fatal Links instead of spending the burst too early.
failureCases: Atlas engages before Pharsa is in range.
The enemy cancels Atlas or avoids the pull.
Pharsa is forced to reposition during the engage window.

reasons: Atlas's Fatal Links can pull enemies into the grouped position Odette wants for AoE channel damage.
Odette can convert Atlas's engage into heavy teamfight damage if the enemy cannot interrupt her.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies first, then Odette channels AoE damage into the grouped targets.
worksBestWhen: Odette casts after enemy interrupts are already used or controlled.
failureCases: The enemy spreads out or uses displacement before Odette follows.

reasons: Atlas can group enemies for Claude's AoE teamfight damage window.
Claude can enter after Atlas has already forced enemy positioning and control responses.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped engage, while Claude follows into the controlled area for AoE marksman damage.
worksBestWhen: Claude waits for Atlas to commit first.
Atlas catches priority targets or forces enemy escape skills.
failureCases: Claude enters before Atlas lands the setup.
The enemy keeps hard CC for Claude.
Atlas engages too deep for Claude to safely follow.

reasons: Atlas pulls enemies together, making it easier for Luo Yi to trigger Yin-Yang Reaction.
Luo Yi adds magic burst and displacement pressure after Atlas starts the fight.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies with Fatal Links, then Luo Yi applies opposite marks to trigger additional AoE control and damage.
worksBestWhen: Luo Yi holds enough charges for Atlas's engage.
Atlas groups multiple enemies close together.
failureCases: The enemy uses immunity or dash tools before Luo Yi's reaction lands.

reasons: Atlas can hold enemies in a grouped area, giving Ixia a clearer angle for fixed-position teamfight damage.
Ixia benefits when Atlas forces enemies to stay in front of her damage zone.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the control window, while Ixia follows if her firing angle is already prepared.
worksBestWhen: Ixia is positioned before Atlas commits.
Atlas pulls enemies into Ixia's damage line rather than away from it.
failureCases: Atlas engages behind terrain or outside Ixia's angle.
Ixia is forced to move and loses the damage window.
The enemy dives Ixia before the setup completes.
